functions.name: date

functions.name: round

functions.description: deprecated: use specialized rounding functions instead (e.g. round up).

functions.name: round up
functions.signature: round up(n: number, scale: number) : number
functions.description: Return n with given scale and rounding mode round up. If at least one of n or scale is null the result is null.

functions.signature: round down(n: number, scale: number) : number
functions.description: Return n with given scale and rounding mode round down. If at least one of n or scale is null the result is null.

functions.signature: round half up(n: number, scale: number) : number
functions.description: Return n with given scale and rounding mode round half up. If at least one of n or scale is null the result is null.

functions.name: round half down
functions.signature: round half down(n: number, scale: number) : number
functions.description: Return n with given scale and rounding mode round up. If at least one of n or scale is null the result is null.

functions.name: floor
functions.description: Return n with given scale and rounding mode flooring. If at least one of n or scale is null the result is null.

functions.name: floor
functions.description: Return n with given scale and rounding mode flooring. If at least one of n or scale is null the result is null.

functions.name: ceiling
functions.description: Return n with given scale and rounding mode ceiling. If at least one of n or scale is null the result is null.

functions.name: ceiling
functions.description: Return n with given scale and rounding mode ceiling. If at least one of n or scale is null the result is null.

functions.description: Splits the string into a list of substrings, breaking at each occurrence of the delimiter pattern.

functions.name: string join
functions.signature: string join(list: list<string>, delimiter: string?) : string
functions.description: return a string which is composed by joining all the string elements from the list parameter, separated by the delimiter. The delimiter can be an empty string. Null elements in the list parameter are ignored. If list is empty, the result is the empty string. If delimiter is null, the string elements are joined without a separator.

functions.name: is

functions.description: return sum of numbers, or null if list is empty

functions.description: return true if any item is true, else false if empty or all items are false, else null

functions.description: return true if any item is true, else false if empty or all items are false, else null

functions.description: flatten nested lists

functions.name: product
functions.description: returns the product of the numbers

functions.name: product
functions.description: returns the product of the numbers

functions.name: median
functions.description: Returns the median element of the list of numbers. I.e., after sorting the list, if the list has an odd number of elements, it returns the middle element. If the list has an even number of elements, returns the average of the two middle elements. If the list is empty, returns null.

functions.name: median
functions.description: Returns the median element of the list of numbers. I.e., after sorting the list, if the list has an odd number of elements, it returns the middle element. If the list has an even number of elements, returns the average of the two middle elements. If the list is empty, returns null.

functions.name: stddev
functions.description: Returns the sample standard deviation of the list of numbers. If the list is empty or if the list contains only one element, the function returns null.

functions.name: stddev
functions.description: Returns the sample standard deviation of the list of numbers. If the list is empty or if the list contains only one element, the function returns null.

functions.name: mode
functions.description: Returns the mode of the list of numbers. If the result contains multiple elements, they are returned in ascending order. If the list is empty, an empty list is returned.

functions.name: mode
functions.description: Returns the mode of the list of numbers. If the result contains multiple elements, they are returned in ascending order. If the list is empty, an empty list is returned.

functions.name: list replace
functions.signature: list replace(list: list<Any>, position: number, newItem: Any) : list<Any>
functions.description: return new list with newItem replaced at position (if position is a number) or return a new list where newItem replaced at all positions where the match function returned true

functions.name: list replace
functions.signature: list replace(list: list<Any>, match: function<Null, Null> -> boolean, newItem: Any) : list<Any>
functions.description: return new list with newItem replaced at position (if position is a number) or return a new list where newItem replaced at all positions where the match function returned true

functions.name: sort
functions.signature: sort(list: list<Any>, precedes: function<Null, Null> -> boolean) : list<Any>
functions.description: Sort a list using an ordering function.

functions.name: get entries
functions.description: produces a list of key,value pairs from a context m

functions.name: get value
functions.signature: get value(m: context, key: string) : Any
functions.description: select the value of the entry named key from context m

functions.name: context
functions.description: Returns a new context that includes all specified entries. If a context item contains additional entries beyond the required "key" and "value" entries, the additional entries are ignored. If a context item is missing the required "key" and "value" entries, the final result is null. See also: get entries() builtin function.

functions.name: context put
functions.signature: context put(context: context, key: string, value: Any) : context
functions.description: (a) Returns a new context that includes the new entry, or overriding the existing value if an entry for the same key already exists in the supplied context parameter. A new entry is added as the last entry of the new context. If overriding an existing entry, the order of the keys maintains the same order as in the original context.

functions.name: context put
functions.signature: context put(context: context, keys: list<string>, value: Any) : context
functions.description: Returns the composite of nested invocations to context put() for each item in keys hierarchy in context. If keys is a list of 1 element, this is equivalent to context put(context, key', value), where key' is the only element in the list keys. If keys is a list of 2 or more elements, this is equivalent of calling context put(context, key', value'), with: key' is the head element in the list keys, value' is the result of invocation of context put(context', keys', value), where: context' is the result of context.key', keys' is the remainder of the list keys without the head element key'. If keys is an empty list or null, the result is null.

functions.name: context merge
functions.description: Returns a new context that includes all entries from the given contexts; if some of the keys are equal, the entries are overriden. The entries are overridden in the same order as specified by the supplied parameter, with new entries added as the last entry in the new context.

functions.name: before
functions.description: Evaluates to true if and only if point1 < point2

functions.name: before
functions.description: Evaluates to true if and only if point < range.start or (point = range.start and not(range.start included) )

functions.name: before
functions.description: Evaluates to true if and only if range.end < point or (range.end = point and not(range.end included) )

functions.name: before
functions.description: Evaluates to true if and only if range1.end < range2.start or (( not(range1.end included) or not(range2.start included)) and range 1 .end = range2.start)

functions.name: after
functions.description: Evaluates to true if and only if point1 > point2

functions.name: after
functions.description: Evaluates to true if and only if point > range.end or (point = range.end and not(range.end included) )

functions.name: after
functions.description: Evaluates to true if and only if range.start > point or (range.start = point and not(range.start included) )

functions.name: after
functions.description: Evaluates to true if and only if range 1 .start > range2.end or (( not(range1 .start included) or not(range2.end included) ) and range 1 .start = range2.end)

functions.name: meets
functions.description: Evaluates to true if and only if range1.end included and range2.start included and range 1 .end = range2.start

functions.name: met by
functions.signature: met by(range1: range<comparable>, range2: range<comparable>) : boolean
functions.description: Evaluates to true if and only if range1.start included and range2.end included and range 1 .start = range2.end

functions.name: overlaps
functions.description: Evaluates to true if and only if (range1.end > range2.start or (range1.end = range2.start and range1.end included and range2.start included)) and (range1.start < range2.end or (range1.start = range2.end and range1.start included and range2.end included))

functions.name: overlaps before
functions.signature: overlaps before(range1: range<comparable>, range2: range<comparable>) : boolean
functions.description: Evaluates to true if and only if (range1.start < range2.start or (range1.start = range2.start and range1.start included and not(range2.start included))) and (range1.end > range2.start or (range1.end = range2.start and range1.end included and range2.start included)) and (range1.end < range2.end or (range1.end = range2.end and (not(range1.end included) or range2.end included )))

functions.name: overlaps after
functions.signature: overlaps after(range1: range<comparable>, range2: range<comparable>) : boolean
functions.description: Evaluates to true if and only if (range2.start < range1.start or (range2.start = range1.start and range2.start included and not( range 1.start included))) and (range2.end > range 1.start or (range2.end = range 1.start and range2.end included and range 1.start included )) and (range2.end < range1.end or (range2.end = range1.end and (not(range2.end included) or range1.end included)))

functions.name: finishes
functions.description: Evaluates to true if and only if range.end included and range.end = point

functions.name: finishes
functions.description: Evaluates to true if and only if range1.end included = range2.end included and range1.end = range2.end and (range1.start > range2.start or (range1.start = range2.start and (not(range1.start included) or range2.start included)))

functions.name: finished by
functions.signature: finished by(range: range<comparable>, point: comparable) : boolean
functions.description: Evaluates to true if and only if range.end included and range.end = point

functions.name: finished by
functions.signature: finished by(range1: range<comparable>, range2: range<comparable>) : boolean
functions.description: Evaluates to true if and only if range1.end included = range2.end included and range1.end = range2.end and (range1.start < range2.start or (range1.start = range2.start and (range1.start included or not(range2.start included))))

functions.name: includes
functions.description: Evaluates to true if and only if (range.start < point and range.end > point) or (range.start = point and range.start included) or (range.end = point and range.end included)

functions.name: includes
functions.description: Evaluates to true if and only if (range1.start < range2.start or (range1.start = range2.start and (range1.start included or not(range2.start included)))) and (range1.end > range2.end or (range1.end = range2.end and (range1.end included or not(range2.end included))))

functions.name: during
functions.description: Evaluates to true if and only if (range.start < point and range.end > point) or (range.start = point and range.start included) or (range.end = point and range.end included)

functions.name: during
functions.description: Evaluates to true if and only if (range2.start < range1.start or (range2.start = range1.start and (range2.start included or not(range1.start included)))) and (range2.end > range1.end or (range2.end = range1.end and (range2.end included or not(range1.end included))))

functions.name: starts
functions.description: Evaluates to true if and only if range.start = point and range.start included

functions.name: starts
functions.description: Evaluates to true if and only if range1.start = range2.start and range1.start included = range2.start included and (range1.end < range2.end or (range1.end = range2.end and (not(range1.end included) or range2.end included)))

functions.name: started by
functions.signature: started by(range: range<comparable>, point: comparable) : boolean
functions.description: Evaluates to true if and only if range.start = point and range.start included

functions.name: started by
functions.signature: started by(range1: range<comparable>, range2: range<comparable>) : boolean
functions.description: Evaluates to true if and only if range1.start = range2.start and range1.start included = range2.start included and (range2.end < range1.end or (range2.end = range1.end and (not(range2.end included) or range1.end included)))

functions.name: coincides
functions.description: Evaluates to true if and only if point1 = point2

functions.name: coincides
functions.description: Evaluates to true if and only if range1.start = range2.start and range1.start included = range2.start included and range1.end = range2.end and range1.end included = range2.end included
